steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The passage names two memorable woodcuts in the opening sentence: Evening at Carl Inn and Lake Basin in the High Sierra. This is the natural subject when the next sentence says “In 2019, ______ exhibited...”

steps.title: Apply pronoun-verb agreement
steps.content:
B uses “they were” — the plural pronoun they directly refers to the two named woodcuts, and were agrees in number.

A and C use singular forms (it/this was) that violate number agreement with the plural antecedent, while D's some is vague and doesn't clearly identify the two specific works.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
